skill.name: mailchimp-automation
skill.description: Automate Mailchimp campaigns, audiences, subscribers, segments, and analytics through Rube MCP with schema lookup before each workflow.
skill.summary: Guide AI assistants through Mailchimp marketing workflows using Rube MCP tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many shell execution findings, but review shows they are Markdown inline tool names and parameter names, not executable Ruby or shell code. The real risk is operational: the skill directs agents to use an external Rube MCP endpoint that can send campaigns, modify subscribers, and read Mailchimp analytics after OAuth authorization.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Service Can Perform Mailchimp Actions
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp and authenticate a Mailchimp connection. This is expected functionality, but it sends requests through an external MCP service and can operate on a live Mailchimp account. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The endpoint and OAuth connection workflow are explicit, and the skill purpose depends on external Mailchimp access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and OAuth connection workflow are explicit. The risk is legitimate integration exposure, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Irreversible Campaign Sending Requires Explicit Approval
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The workflow includes MAILCHIMP_SEND_CAMPAIGN and warns that sending is irreversible. A mistaken or unauthorized invocation could send email to an audience, so human approval is required before live sends. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The skill directly documents immediate sending and the irreversible nature of this action.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The live-send workflow and irreversible warning are explicitly documented. The concern is operational impact rather than malware.

medium_findings.title: Subscriber Data Modification and Analytics Access
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill can add, update, search, and list subscribers, and it can retrieve campaign reports and activity. These actions may expose or modify personal data in Mailchimp, so users need correct authorization and scope control. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The documented tool sequences include subscriber upserts, searches, member lists, reports, and per-subscriber activity.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data access and modification operations are clearly listed. They are normal Mailchimp tasks but involve sensitive contact data.

medium_findings.title: MD5 Subscriber Hash Is Not Protective Cryptography
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The skill instructs users to compute an MD5 hash of a lowercase email for Mailchimp subscriber_hash values. This appears to follow Mailchimp API requirements, not to protect secrets, but MD5 email hashes should still be treated as personal data. Confidence: 0.72. Confidence reasoning: The MD5 usage is explicit and context shows it is an API identifier rather than a security control.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The weak hash use is explicit, but the surrounding text indicates a Mailchimp-required identifier. This is privacy-sensitive, not evidence of malicious cryptography.

low_findings.title: Markdown Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution findings are inline Markdown references to Rube and Mailchimp tool names, parameters, or examples. No executable shell, Ruby backtick expression, or user-input command construction was found in SKILL.md. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The flagged locations are prose, lists, and tables inside a Markdown skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is Markdown documentation with inline code formatting, not executable code. The file contains no script runner or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Mailchimp Lookups
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged reconnaissance-like entries refer to Mailchimp list, segment, campaign, and member lookup workflows. They inspect Mailchimp account data after authorization and do not enumerate the local system. Confidence: 0.89. Confidence reasoning: The surrounding context is Mailchimp API workflow documentation, with no host reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines describe Mailchimp object lookup or validation. No local host or network scanning behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External URL Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill directs users to configure https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is necessary for the integration, but it means user actions and Mailchimp OAuth flows depend on a third-party service. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The URL is directly documented, and no evidence suggests hidden exfiltration beyond the stated integration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external service URL is explicit and intentional. It is a publication warning rather than a block.

content.value_statement: Managing campaigns, subscribers, and reports across Mailchimp can require many precise API steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows with the right tool order and safety checks.
actual_capabilities: Connects Mailchimp workflows through Rube MCP after tool schema lookup.
Creates campaigns, sets content, sends tests, sends campaigns, or schedules delivery.
Lists audiences, searches members, retrieves subscriber details, and manages segments.
Adds or updates subscribers and supports bulk segment membership operations.
Retrieves campaign reports, link click details, and subscriber activity data.
Documents Mailchimp parameter quirks, pagination rules, and required identifiers.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP server and an authorized Mailchimp connection.
Cannot bypass Mailchimp permissions, rate limits, compliance rules, or OAuth failures.
Live campaign sending is irreversible and needs explicit user approval.
Tool schemas may change, so the skill requires schema lookup before each workflow.

use_cases.title: Launch a newsletter campaign
use_cases.description: Create a campaign, assign the audience, add content, send a test, and schedule delivery after approval.

use_cases.title: Maintain audience records
use_cases.description: Search subscribers, update merge fields, apply tags, and manage segment membership with fewer manual steps.

use_cases.title: Review campaign performance
use_cases.description: Fetch reports, compare opens and clicks, inspect link activity, and review subscriber engagement.

prompt_templates.title: List my audiences
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Mailchimp automation skill to list my available audiences and summarize member counts. Search Rube tools first.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a test campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a draft Mailchimp campaign for the selected audience, set the subject and preview text, add provided HTML, and send only a test email.

prompt_templates.title: Update subscribers safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Find these subscribers in Mailchimp, upsert missing records as pending, update merge fields, and report every planned change before applying it.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze campaign engagement
prompt_templates.prompt: Find campaigns sent last month, fetch detailed reports, identify the top clicked links, and summarize subscriber activity patterns.

output_examples.input: Show me the active audiences and recent campaign status.
output_examples.output: A concise account summary with audience names, subscribed member counts, recent campaign titles, send status, and any missing report data.

output_examples.input: Prepare a product update campaign and send a test to reviewers.
output_examples.output: A draft campaign confirmation with audience, subject, sender, preview text, content status, and test email recipients. No live send occurs.

output_examples.input: Analyze click performance for the spring sale campaign.
output_examples.output: A performance summary with opens, clicks, bounces, unsubscribes, top links, and recommended follow-up segments.

best_practices: Search Rube tools before every workflow so parameter schemas are current.
Send a test email and get explicit approval before any live campaign send.
Use pagination and field filters to control response size for large audiences.
anti_patterns: Do not send a live campaign without reviewer approval and compliance checks.
Do not treat MD5 subscriber hashes as anonymized or secure identifiers.
Do not omit pagination when reading members, campaigns, reports, or activity.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Mailchimp API key?
faq.answer: The skill expects Mailchimp access through Rube MCP OAuth, not a manually entered API key.

faq.question: Can it send real email campaigns?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents live send and scheduling workflows, so users should require explicit approval before sending.

faq.question: Can it manage subscribers?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports listing, searching, adding, updating, tagging, and segment membership workflows.

faq.question: Can it analyze reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide report retrieval, link click review, and subscriber activity analysis.

faq.question: Why does it mention MD5?
faq.answer: Mailchimp uses an MD5 hash of the lowercase email as a subscriber identifier for several endpoints.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses an external MCP service and can change live Mailchimp data.
